__fixture__.name: policy_locked_source_badge_group
__fixture__.scenario: A local policy-managed settings source has a dirty draft in the tab, but policy blocks save. The fixture proves dirty state does not imply write authority and policy lock does not erase the canonical source relation.

source_of_truth_relation.relation_label: The workspace settings file is source-controlled, but a policy rule controls whether this field can be saved.

write_authority.authority_summary: The draft is local dirty state, but save authority is blocked by the active policy rule.

dirty_state.dirty_summary: The editor owns unsaved local text, but policy prevents committing it to the canonical file.

freshness.freshness_summary: The policy lock and file identity are current for the active policy epoch.

reason_strip.cause_plain_language: A workspace policy locks this settings field against local saves.
reason_strip.scope_plain_language: The lock applies to the dirty draft for this settings file in the current workspace.
reason_strip.boundary_plain_language: The protected boundary is policy write authority, not the existence of a dirty buffer.
reason_strip.safe_next_step_plain_language: Request approval or discard the dirty draft before trying to save.

canonical_source_relation_row.source_label: Active policy rule controls the save path for this settings file.

next_safe_action.action_summary: Requests scoped approval before any dirty draft can be committed.

export_and_accessibility.accessible_name: Policy locked settings file. This tab has a dirty draft, but policy blocks direct save.
